Physikalisch-Meteorologisches Observatorium Davos, World Radiation Center
Federal Office of Meteorology and Climatology MeteoSwiss

gmd:deliveryPoint.gco:CharacterString: Station Aérologique MeteoSchweiz Les Invuardes Case postale 316

gmd:organisationName.gco:CharacterString: Federal Office of Meteorology and Climatology MeteoSwiss
Physikalisch-Meteorologisches Observatorium Davos, World Radiation Center

gmd:deliveryPoint.gco:CharacterString: Physikalisch-Meteorologisches Observatorium Davos, World Radiation Center Dorfstrasse 33

gmd:organisationName.gco:CharacterString: Federal Office of Meteorology and Climatology MeteoSwiss

wmdr:Description.wmdr:description: The Physikalisch-Meteorologisches Observatorium is located the northeaster end of the Davos valley in the Swiss alps. The city of Davos is extending to the southwest of the site. [this description has been copied from AERONET Site Information Database of NASA]

The ozone observations which started in Arosa (0-20008-0-ARO) in 1926 have been transferred to Davos. Arosa is located approximately 12 km south-west of Davos and about 200 m higher. The parallel observation period (with instruments of Arosa being temporarily deployed in Davos) started on 22 Nov 2011. 4 of the 6 instruments (2 Brewers 2 Dobsons) were definitively transferred to Davos on 28 Feb 2018. The two remaining instruments (1 Brewer and 1 Dobson) continued observations in Arosa until 18 Feb 2021 when they were also transferred to Davos and the ozone observations at Arosa definitively stopped. Since then, the ozone time series is continued from Davos.
